india_trends.title: Sebi panel weighs risks as MCX pitches weekly bullion index options
india_trends.summary: A regulatory panel is discussing a proposal by MCX to launch weekly options on its bullion index Bulldex. While the move may boost volumes on the exchange, concerns over retail losses and risks in weekly options could make it difficult to get Sebi’s approval.

india_trends.title: RBI a net buyer of US dollars in February
india_trends.summary: The Reserve Bank of India became a net buyer of $7.4 billion in February, a shift from January's net sales. This likely benefited from a stronger rupee after the India-US trade deal announcement. The real effective exchange rate (REER) for the rupee decreased in March, indicating undervaluation and

india_trends.title: Oil Rises Fifth Day as Iran Talks Impasse Adds to Uncertainty
india_trends.summary: Oil climbed for a fifth day, its longest string of gains since January, as uncertainty over talks between the US and Iran threatened to further delay flows from the Persian Gulf.

india_trends.title: SP Group to raise Rs 25,400 crore via bonds, loans
india_trends.summary: Shapoorji Pallonji Group is nearing the closure of a ₹25,400 crore debt raise by May 15, featuring annual coupons around 18.75%. This fundraising, part of Project Ascent, will utilize USD bonds and bespoke loans to refinance existing debt, including bonds secured against its Tata Sons stake.

india_trends.title: Yuan to top Yen in currency options trading: LCH
india_trends.summary: The yuan is poised to surpass the yen as the second-most traded currency against the dollar in foreign-exchange options, according to LCH. This shift, expected by 2028, reflects growing yuan internationalization and market demand for currency diversification beyond the dollar.

india_trends.title: Is India's retail investor story losing its stickiness?
india_trends.summary: &nbsp;After six years of relentless buying, India’s mom-and-pop investors are retreating to the sidelines. As ‘transient’ traders replace the long-term cohorts, sophisticated desks are moving in to anchor the market.

india_trends.title: Rupee breaches 94/$ for first time in April

Higher crude oil prices also contributed to the Rupee's weakening. The currency closed at 94.11 against the US Dollar. Bren
